quote_of_day.text: I have learned more about programming and computers from certain streamers (Alexey kutepov, Casey Muratori etc) and books than from any paid kalej course online or offline.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of caste hatred moved into the board's northern sector, bringing with it a cold snap of vitriolic comments about Chuttad Pradesh and Peehar's socioeconomic issues. Anon questioned the regions' plight, citing corruption, tribalism, and crime as factors.

A scattered drizzle of gang-rape allegations fell across the board, sparking a heat wave of reactions. Anon claimed Muslims gang-raped a Dalit, drawing responses that ranged from disgust to schadenfreude.

In contrast, a high-pressure system of technical curiosity brought clear skies to parts of the board. Anon recommended learning about computer processors, describing it as a fun and enlightening experience.

Nearby, a fog of nostalgia rolled in, as another anon mused on Microsoft's 'Winvidea' era, sharing links to related articles.

articles.title: Anon Questions Chuttad Pradesh and Peehar's Socioeconomic Issues
articles.body:
Anon opened a discussion on the perceived subhuman conditions of Chuttad Pradesh and Peehar, describing their inhabitants as 'ugly, uncouth, bad-natured people' living in 'low trust societies plagued with corruption, tribalism and crime.' The original poster asked what causes these issues.

One respondent attributed the problems to 'dysgenic fertility' caused by 'comfy conditions,' contrasting these regions with the 'harsh winters and terrain' of the Himalayan states, which they claimed created 'high trust societies.' Another anon pointed to the 'Gangu faustian spirit' as a positive force in the country, while criticizing 'abbos' for their perceived negative impacts.

Caste was cited as a significant factor by one anon, stating 'only one word. Caste.' Another respondent noted that 'UP/Bihar is filled with low castes (like 98%).

That's why it's a shithole.' A different perspective suggested that 'reproduction' is the primary issue, with one anon bluntly stating '>>4950 >why is upee and peeehar degen Reproduction.' The discussion also touched on genetics, with one anon attributing the problems to 'genes.' Others made comparative statements, such as 'UP and Bihar mogs south anyday, stay malding.' The conversation remained focused on perceived socioeconomic and genetic factors, without exploring potential solutions or policy changes.

The thread included several tangential comments, such as an image reference and a personal jab, but these did not divert the discussion from its core topics.

Anon's query about Chuttad Pradesh and Peehar sparked a range of responses, but no clear consensus emerged on the causes of their socioeconomic challenges.

articles.title: Anon claims Muslims gang-raped Dalit, sparks reactions
articles.body:
Anon posted a message claiming that Muslims gang-raped a Dalit, using derogatory terms for both groups. The poster seemed to mock the situation, saying Muslims should continue doing this until Dalits stop begging for seats and water from 'UCs'.

Another anon replied, saying that those responsible would get away with it due to a lack of law protecting men in India. This anon also mentioned that the Indian Penal Code does not recognize men as capable of being raped.

A respondent suggested that Muslims targeted a Dalit because they had no shortage of Hindu 'concubines'. Another anon joked about Muslims having to rape a 'dalit shitskin' when they could have a 'qt mulli'.

One anon addressed a 'dalit vermin', telling them to shut up and accusing them of being unable to think outside of rape and killing.

A different respondent expressed a desire to see a 'baman girl' in a similar situation, suggesting it would be entertaining to see her 'metldown'.

Anon discussed sharing the claim on social media platforms like Instagram, Reddit, and 4chan to 'troll dalits to death'. Others referenced 'love jihad news' and interfaith relationships. One respondent expressed support for consensual relationships between Muslims and others.

The thread included various reactions, with some anons using derogatory language and others making jokes. One anon simply said 'Kys' in response to the claim, while another used the term 'cuckpilled'.

The discussion was marked by a tone of mockery and hostility towards Dalits and Muslims.

articles.title: Streamer Scam Fears
articles.body:
Anon talked about a live stream they watched, claiming it turned out to be a scam when they asked for the streamer's location. Another anon responded, seemingly unfazed, suggesting the streamer was likely of high caste and therefore not a problem.

This anon told the OP they had no reason to be tense unless they were of a lower caste.

A third anon warned the OP not to share the streamer's link, fearing other users would flirt with her and she would fall for them, leading to the OP getting cheated on.

The OP shared more details, saying they had revealed their city and college to the streamer, who claimed to be in 12th grade and preparing for the NEET exam.

One anon downplayed the risk, comparing it to a more serious breach by the DRDO chief, implying that a scam by the streamer wouldn't have significant consequences.

Later, another anon advised the OP to be cautious, stating that one should never engage in such interactions without financial independence and characterizing the streamers as "bad women".

Anon also jokingly asked about the language used in the conversation, and another anon simply asked the OP to share the streamer's link.

Some anons made light of the situation, with one saying the worst that could happen was minimal, while another joked about the streamer's potential actions, saying they would use the OP, break their heart, and then make them go schizophrenic.

Anon also made a brief comment calling the situation "fake and gay" and saying they were saged.

articles.title: Anon recommends learning about computer processors
articles.body:
Anon started a thread saying it's fun to learn about how processors work. They mentioned that a relevant resource is available online as a PDF, but couldn't upload it due to file restrictions.

Another anon suggested that proper knowledge of C/C++, assembly languages, and system software is preferable for understanding the material.

A third anon dismissed self-study, saying it's not useful unless one takes a proper computer architecture course.

The discussion turned to the credibility of teachers and resources, with some anons praising online streamers and books over paid courses. One anon joked that GPT and cliff notes are the best teachers.

articles.title: Anon muses on Microsoft's 'Winvidea' era
articles.body:
Anon posted links to Microsoft's Surface Laptop Ultra and an article about Nvidia's RTX Spark on an imageboard.

The poster dubbed this 'the era of Winvidea.' Anon noted RTX Spark lets users render large 3D scenes, edit 12K video, generate 4K AI videos, and run large language models locally.

Another Anon mentioned the tech could enable running a 1 trillion parameter AI model locally. Anon also speculated about potential hardware backdoors in new chipsets made in an era of accepted CIA surveillance. One respondent simply stated 'Nvidia won.'
